compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 9 advisory). Triage — all valid, folded into implementation: (1) f7ca28: place default-assignee fallback at TOP of claim_compute, before the parent-cascade recursion, so cascaded parent claims inherit the default. (2) ffea27: use None sentinel for omitted --assignee; explicit --assignee '' clears (explicit wins), only omitted falls back to default. (3) f9ea30: claim_cli captures claim_compute's return and uses result['assignee'] for JSON + report suffix. (4) f27285: claim writes a LOCAL assignee (EDIT event); resolution to a Jira accountId happens at reconcile (co-landing unify fix 544e). Default must be Jira-resolvable -> configuring joeoakhart@navapbc.com (the resolvable email, not a bare handle); jira.user kept separate (auth user vs work-attribution) but documented as usually the same. (5) f2460f: canonical env name = REBAR_DEFAULT_ASSIGNEE in _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES. (6) cascade path covered by tests. RED tests precede implementation.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Claiming with no `--assignee` (or with a non-Jira handle) leaves tickets effectively unassigned — and a bare handle like `joe` gets cleared by Jira sync (see bug `balm-feast-mica`). A configurable default assignee lets a checkout/agent set a sensible Jira-resolvable identity once, so claims attribute work correctly by default.

## What
Add a `default_assignee` config key (config file + env override, following the existing rebar config pattern), and make `claim` fall back to it when `--assignee` is unspecified/empty.

## Scope
**Config** (`src/rebar/config.py`):
- Add `default_assignee: str = ""` to the existing `TicketConfig` dataclass (the `[tickets]` section) — it is a ticket-workflow setting, not a Jira-connector setting.
- Wire the env override following the established pattern: either the auto-derived `REBAR_TICKET_DEFAULT_ASSIGNEE`, or add an explicit entry to `_CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES` if a nicer name is wanted. Precedence stays CLI > env > project config > user config > default.
- Register the key in `_SECTIONS` so typo-detection (`unknown key … ignored`) and `env_overrides()` pick it up.

**Claim** (`src/rebar/_commands/claim.py`):
- In `claim_compute()`, BEFORE `txn.claim_core(...)` (~line 140), if `assignee` is empty, fall back to `config.load_config(repo_root).ticket.default_assignee`.
- The existing `claim_core` (`txn.py`) already writes the assignee as an EDIT event only when non-empty — unchanged.
- Behavior: explicit `--assignee` always wins; empty + configured default → default is used; empty + no default → unchanged (no assignee), as today.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `[tickets] default_assignee = "<id>"` in the config file is read and applied.
- [ ] Env override (`REBAR_TICKET_DEFAULT_ASSIGNEE` or the chosen canonical name) overrides the file value; precedence CLI > env > project > user > default holds.
- [ ] `claim <id>` with no `--assignee` uses the configured default; `claim <id> --assignee X` still uses X (explicit wins); empty + no default behaves exactly as today.
- [ ] The key is in the known-key set (no "unknown key" warning) and documented in `docs/config.md`.
- [ ] Recommend (doc note) that the default be a Jira-resolvable identity (email/accountId) so it survives reconcile — ties to bug `balm-feast-mica`.
- [ ] Tests: config load (file + env + precedence), and claim fallback (explicit / default / none) including the cascade claim path.

## Affected Files (initial)
- `src/rebar/config.py` (`TicketConfig`, `_SECTIONS`, `_CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES`)
- `src/rebar/_commands/claim.py` (`claim_compute` fallback)
- `docs/config.md`
compiled_state.title: Add default_assignee config (file + env override) and have claim use it when --assignee is omitted
